steps.title: Find constant b using the initial condition
steps.content:
At $t = 0$, the object is dropped from a height of 5.76 feet. Substitute into $h(t) = -16t^2 + b$:
$$h(0) = -16(0)^2 + b = 5.76$$
Therefore, $b = 5.76$.

steps.title: Set up the equation for when the object hits the ground
steps.content:
The object hits the ground when its height equals zero. Set $h(t) = 0$:
$$-16t^2 + 5.76 = 0$$

steps.title: Solve for time t
steps.content:
Rearrange the equation to isolate $t^2$:
$$16t^2 = 5.76$$
$$t^2 = \frac{5.76}{16} = 0.36$$
Take the positive square root (since time must be positive):
$$t = \sqrt{0.36} = 0.6 \text{ seconds}$$
This can also be expressed as $\frac{3}{5}$ seconds.
The answer is 0.6,3/5.
